overview.title: Product Architecture & Portal Architecture
overview.subtitle: The product layer answers "what we sell," while the portal layer answers "who uses it and where actions happen." Together they form a complete, operable system.
overview.description: MOTA's architecture has two orthogonal dimensions: the product architecture defines three sellable service layers (Subscription / Report / Outreach), and the portal architecture defines three user-facing interfaces (public portal / customer dashboard / admin dashboard).

highlights.label: Three-Layer Stack
highlights.desc: From continuous scanning to per-report research to pay-per-performance outreach — each layer is independently sellable

highlights.label: Three Portal Separation
highlights.desc: Public portal for visitors, dashboard for customers, admin for operations — same data foundation

product-arch.title: Product Architecture (What we sell)
product-arch.subtitle: A three-layer product stack from continuous scanning to performance-based outreach. Each layer is independently sellable or combinable.

layers.name: Project Subscription
layers.desc: The continuous scanning and monitoring layer that handles platform coverage, keyword quotas, and task scheduling.

layers.name: Market Intelligence Report
layers.desc: Aggregates mentions, pain_points, and validations into sellable research assets.

layers.name: Reply Outreach
layers.desc: Turns highly relevant mentions into community replies, click events, and real revenue.

portal-arch.title: Portal Architecture (Who operates it)
portal-arch.subtitle: Same data foundation, three completely different workspaces for three user roles.

portals.goal: Understand the product value and convert into signups
Manage projects, replies, reports, bills, and performance tracking
Monitor tasks, review replies, manage billing, and run the partner ecosystem
